title: Methods for autocatalytic genome editing and neutralizing autocatalytic genome editing
abstract: The invention provides methods for autocatalytic genome editing based on genomic integration of a construct containing multiple elements. More specifically, the invention provides a method for autocatalytic genome editing or for deleting or neutralizing autocatalytic genome editing based on the CRISPR/Cas9 system, and methods of use thereof, in animals, humans, and plants for eliminating pathogens, targeting suppression of crop pests, strategies to combat virus (e.g., HIV) and other diseases (e.g., cancer) caused by retrovirus, as well as to generate, and reverse, homozygous mutations that are transmitted to nearly all offspring.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS  WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. A method of reversibly introducing a nucleic acid sequence into a genome, the method comprising genomically integrating a mutagenic chain reaction (MCR) element from a MCR construct into a cell or organism, wherein the MCR element comprises: (a) at least one sequence encoding at least one guide polynucleotide, wherein the at least one sequence encoding at least one guide polynucleotide is genomically integrated in the cell or organism; and (b) a gene encoding an endonuclease. 2. The method of claim 1 , wherein the MCR element comprises a sequence of at least 5 kb. 3. The method of claim 1, wherein the MCR element comprises a sequence of at least 10 kb. 4. The method of claim 1 , wherein the MCR element comprises a sequence of at least 17 kb. 5. The method of claim 1, wherein the MCR element comprises a sequence of at least 50 kb. 6. The method of any of the preceding claims, wherein the method is further neutralized using a neutralizing chain reaction. 7. A method of neutralizing a mutagenic chain reaction (MCR) element in a cell or organism, the method comprising genomically integrating a neutralizing chain reaction (NCR) element from an NCR construct into the cell or organism, wherein: the MCR element comprises: (a) at least one sequence encoding at least one guide polynucleotide, wherein the at least one sequence encoding at least one guide polynucleotide is genomically integrated in the cell or organism; and (b) a gene encoding an endonuclease; the NCR element comprises: (a) at least one sequence encoding at least one guide polynucleotide directing cleavage within or on both sides of the MCR element; and 

 (b) at least one sequence encoding at least two guide polynucleotides directing cleavage within or outside of the MCR element or no gene encoding an endonuclease; and the NCR construct comprises: (a) the NCR element; and (b) homology arms flanking the at least one guide polynucleotide that directly abut the endonuclease cut site(s) determined by the at least one guide polynucleotide. 8. The method of any of the preceeding claims, wherein the guide polynucleotides are guide RNAs. 9. The method of any of the preceding claims, wherein the endonuclease is a Cas protein. 10. The method of any of the preceding claims wherein the endonuclease is Cas9. 11. The method of any of the preceding claims, wherein the cell or organism is a cell. 12. The method of any of the preceding claims, wherein the cell or organism is an organism. 13. The method of any of the preceding claims, wherein the genomically integrating comprises genomically integrating into a chromosome of the cell or organism. 14. The method of any of the preceding claims, wherein the gene encoding an endonuclease is genomically integrated in the cell or organism. 15. The method of any of the preceding claims, wherein the gene encoding an endonuclease is not genomically integrated in the cell. 16. The method of any of the preceding claims, wherein the gene encoding an endonuclease is located on a plasmid or artificial chromosome. 17. The method of any of the preceding claims, wherein the at least one sequence encoding at least one guide polynucleotide in the MCR element is genomically integrated in the cell or organism. 18. The method of any of the preceding claims, wherein the MCR element is genomically integrated in the cell or organism. 19. The method of any of the preceding claims, wherein the method further comprises deletion of the gene encoding the endonuclease from the genome. 20. The method of any of the preceding claims, wherein the method further comprises deletion of the at least one sequence encoding at least one guide polynucleotide in the MCR element from the genome. 21. The method of any of the preceding claims, wherein the method further comprises deletion of the MCR element from the genome. 22. The method of any of the preceding claims, wherein the method further comprises disruption of the gene encoding the endonuclease. 23. The method of Claim 22, wherein the disruption of the gene encoding the endonuclease in the genome comprises a deletion, insertion, or mutation of at least one amino acid of the endonuclease. 24. The method of Claim 7, wherein directing cleavage within or on both sides of the MCR element comprises directing cleavage on the same allele as the MCR element. 25. The method of Claim 7, wherein the NCR construct does not comprise a gene encoding an endonuclease. 26. The method of Claim 7, wherein the NCR element does not comprise a gene encoding an endonuclease. 27. The method of Claim 7, wherein the at least one sequence encoding at least one guide polynucleotide in the NCR element comprises a different sequence than the at least one sequence encoding at least one guide polynucleotide in the MCR element. 28. The method of Claim 7, wherein the at least one sequence encoding at least one guide polynucleotide in the NCR element directs cleavage within the MCR element. 29. The method of Claim 7, wherein the at least one sequence encoding at least one guide polynucleotide in the NCR element directs cleavage within the gene encoding the endonuclease. 30. The method of Claim 7, wherein the at least one sequence encoding at least one guide polynucleotide in the NCR element directs cleavage within the at least one sequence encoding at least one guide polynucleotide in the MCR element. 31. The method of Claim 7, wherein the at least one sequence encoding at least one guide polynucleotide in the NCR element directs cleavage on both sides of the MCR element. 32. The method of Claim 7, wherein the at least one sequence encoding at least one guide polynucleotide in the NCR element directs cleavage on both sides of the gene encoding the endonuclease. 33. The method of Claim 7, wherein the at least one sequence encoding at least one guide polynucleotide in the NCR element directs cleavage on both sides of the at least one sequence encoding at least one guide polynucleotide in the MCR element. 34. The method of Claim 7, wherein the NCR construct and/or NCR element comprises one guide polynucleotide. 35. The method of Claim 20, wherein the one guide polynucleotide directs one cleavage site. 36. The method of Claim 20, wherein the one guide polynucleotide directs cleavage within the MCR element. 37. The method of Claim 20, wherein the one guide polynucleotide directs cleavage within the gene encoding the endonuclease. 38. The method of Claim 20, wherein the one guide polynucleotide directs cleavage within the at least one sequence encoding at least one guide polynucleotide in the MCR element. 39. The method of Claim 20, wherein the one guide polynucleotide directs two cleavage sites. 40. The method of Claim 20, wherein the one guide polynucleotide directs cleavage on both sides of the endonuclease. 41. The method of Claim 20, wherein the one guide polynucleotide directs cleavage on both sides of the at least one sequence encoding at least one guide polynucleotide in the MCR element. 42. The method of Claim 20, wherein the one guide polynucleotide directs cleavage on both sides of the MCR element. 43. The method of Claim 1, wherein the NCR construct and/or NCR element comprises two guide polynucleotides. 44. The method of Claim 43, wherein the two guide polynucleotides direct two cleavage sites. 45. The method of Claim 43, wherein the two guide polynucleotides direct cleavage within the MCR element. 46. The method of Claim 43, wherein the two guide polynucleotides direct cleavage within the gene encoding the endonuclease. 47. The method of Claim 43, wherein the two guide polynucleotides direct cleavage within the at least one sequence encoding at least one guide polynucleotide in the MCR element. 48. The method of Claim 43, wherein the two guide polynucleotides direct cleavage on both sides of the gene encoding the endonuclease. 49. The method of Claim 43, wherein the two guide polynucleotides direct cleavage on both sides of the at least one sequence encoding at least one guide polynucleotide in the MCR element. 50. The method of Claim 43, wherein the two guide polynucleotides direct cleavage on both sides of the MCR element. 51. The method of Claim 7, wherein the at least one sequence encoding at least two guide polynucleotides in the NCR element comprises at least two sequences encoding at least two guide polynucleotides. 52. The method of Claim 7, wherein the NCR element is genomically integrated using homology directed repair. 53. The method of Claim 7, wherein the NCR element is not genomically integrated using non-homologous end joining. 54. The method of Claim 7, wherein the NCR element is genomically integrated with an efficiency of at least 25%. 55. The method of Claim 7, wherein the NCR element is genomically integrated with an efficiency of at least 50%. 56. The method of Claim 7, wherein the NCR element is genomically integrated with an efficiency of at least 75%. 57. The method of Claim 7, wherein the NCR construct is located on a plasmid. 58. The method of Claim 7, wherein the NCR construct is located on a chromosome. 59. The method of Claim 7, wherein the homology arms in the NCR construct are located on a plasmid. 60. The method of Claim 7, wherein the homology arms in the NCR construct are located on a chromosome. 61. The method of Claim 7, wherein the homology arms in the NCR construct are at least 50 nucleotides in length. 62. The method of Claim 7, wherein the homology arms in the NCR construct are at least 100 nucleotides in length. 63. The method of Claim 7, wherein the MCR element is located on a first copy of a chromosome and the NCR element is located on a second copy of a chromosome. 64. The method of Claim 7, wherein the NCR element further comprises a corrected recoded gene or cis-regulatory element that is not cut by the at least one guide polynucleotide in the MCR element. 65. The method of Claim 7, wherein the NCR element further comprises a corrected effector cassette. 66. The method of Claim 7, further comprising restoring a genetic function of a locus mutated by the MCR element. 67. The method of Claim 7, wherein the NCR construct is injected as a DNA plasmid into a germline of an organism to obtain a transgenic organism. 68. The method of Claim 7, further comprising generating homozygous mutations in the cell or organism. 69. The method of Claim 7, further comprising genomically integrating the NCR element into both copies of a chromosome of the cell or organism. 70. The method of Claim 7, further comprising propagating the NCR element via the germline to offspring of the organism. 71. The method of Claim 7, wherein the NCR construct is introduced into somatic cells in the organism. 72. The method of Claim 71, further comprising spreading the NCR element to other cells within the organism. 73. The method of Claim 7, wherein the NCR construct is injected as a DNA plasmid into a germline or introduced via DNA plasmid or viral expression vector into 

 somatic cells of the organism to obtain transgenic organisms resulting in homozygous or nearly fully converted germline mutations. 74. The method of Claim 7, wherein the NCR construct is introduced using a plasmid or viral expression vector. 75. The method of Claim 7, wherein the organism is an animal, human, microorganism, insect, plant, or any combination thereof. 76. The method of Claim 7, wherein the organism is a model organism. 77. The method of Claim 7, wherein the organism is a virus, prokaryote, eukaryote, protist, fungus, invertebrate animal, vertebrate animal, microorganism, pathogen, agriculture pest, or any combination thereof. 78. The method of Claim 7, wherein the cell is from a virus, prokaryote, eukaryote, protist, fungus, invertebrate animal, vertebrate animal, microorganism, pathogen, agriculture pest, or any combination thereof. 79. A construct for neutralizing autocatalytic genome editing occurring due to prior integration of a mutagenic chain reaction (MCR) element, the construct comprising: (a) at least one sequence encoding at least one guide polynucleotide directing cleavage within or on both sides of the MCR element; (b) homology arms flanking the at least one guide polynucleotide that directly abut the endonuclease cut site(s) determined by the at least one guide polynucleotide; and (c) at least one sequence encoding at least two guide polynucleotides directing cleavage within or outside of the MCR element or no gene encoding an endonuclease; wherein the MCR element comprises: (a) at least one sequence encoding at least one guide polynucleotide, wherein the at least one sequence encoding at least one guide polynucleotide is genomically integrated in a cell or organism; and (b) a gene encoding an endonuclease. 80. The construct of Claim 79, wherein the guide polynucleotides are guide RNAs. 81. The construct of Claim 79, wherein the endonuclease is a Cas protein. 82. The construct of Claim 81, wherein the Cas protein is Cas9. 83. A method of genomically integrating a neutralizing chain reaction (NCR) element into a cell or organism, the method comprising: introducing into the cell or organism an NCR construct comprising: (a) at least one sequence encoding at least one guide polynucleotide directing cleavage within or on both sides of a mutagenic chain reaction (MCR) element; (b) homology arms flanking the at least one guide polynucleotide that directly abut the endonuclease cut site(s) determined by the at least one guide polynucleotide; and (c) at least one sequence encoding at least two guide polynucleotides directing cleavage within or outside of the MCR element or no gene encoding an endonuclease; and genomically integrating an NCR element comprising: (a) at least one sequence encoding at least one guide polynucleotide directing cleavage within or on both sides of the MCR element; and (b) at least one sequence encoding at least two guide polynucleotides directing cleavage within or outside of the MCR element or no gene encoding an endonuclease; wherein the MCR element comprises: (a) at least one sequence encoding at least one guide polynucleotide; and (b) a gene encoding an endonuclease; and wherein the cell or organism comprises an endonuclease or a gene encoding an endonuclease. 84. The method of Claim 83, wherein the guide polynucleotides are guide RNAs. 85. The method of Claim 83, wherein the endonuclease is a Cas protein. 86. The method of Claim 85, wherein the Cas protein is Cas9. 87. The method of Claim 83, wherein the cell or organism is a cell. 88. The method of Claim 83, wherein the cell or organism is an organism. 89. The method of Claim 83, wherein the cell or organism does not comprise the MCR element. 90. The method of Claim 83, wherein the cell or organism comprises the MCR element. 91. The method of Claim 83, wherein the NCR construct is introduced using a plasmid or viral expression vector. 92. The method of Claim 83, wherein the NCR construct does not comprise a gene encoding an endonuclease. 93. The method of Claim 83, wherein the NCR element does not comprise a gene encoding an endonuclease. 94. The method of Claim 83, wherein the organism is a model organism. 95. The method of Claim 83, wherein the organism is a virus, prokaryote, eukaryote, protist, fungus, invertebrate animal, vertebrate animal, microorganism, pathogen, agriculture pest, or any combination thereof. 96. The method of Claim 83, wherein the cell is from a virus, prokaryote, eukaryote, protist, fungus, invertebrate animal, vertebrate animal, microorganism, pathogen, agriculture pest, or any combination thereof. 97. A method for autocatalytic genome editing, the method comprising genomically integrating a mutagenic chain reaction (MCR) element from an MCR construct into a cell or organism, wherein: the MCR element comprises: (a) a gene encoding an endonuclease, (b) at least one sequence encoding at least one guide polynucleotide, and (c) an effector cassette; and the MCR construct comprises: (d) the MCR element; and (e) homology arms flanking the MCR element, wherein the homology arms directly abut the endonuclease cut site(s) determined by the at least one guide polynucleotide. 98. The method of claim 97, wherein the endonuclease is a Cas protein. 99. The method of claim 98, wherein the Cas protein is Cas9. 100. The method of claim 97, wherein the guide polynucleotide once expressed binds to the endonuclease and directs site directed cleavage of the genome at a specific site. 101. The method of claim 97, wherein the sequence encoding at least one guide polynucleotide is under a control of a separate promoter. 102. The method of claim 101, wherein the separate promoter is an RNA-polymerase-I or III promoter. 103. The method of claim 97, wherein the MCR construct is injected as a DNA plasmid into a germline of the organism to obtain a transgenic organism. 104. The method of claim 103, wherein homozygous mutations are created wherein the transgenic organism carrying the inserted construct on one copy of a chromosome from which it spreads to another chromosome. 105. The method of claim 103, wherein mutations are created wherein the transgenic organism carrying the inserted construct is propagated via the germline to offspring. 106. The method of claim 97, wherein the MCR construct is introduced into somatic cells in an organism so that the construct can be spread to other cells within that organism. 107. The method of claim 106, wherein the MCR construct is introduced using a plasmid or viral expression vector. 108. The method of claim 97, wherein the organism is an animal, human, or plant. 109. The method of claim 97, wherein the organism is a model organism. 110. The method of claim 97, wherein the organism is a virus, prokaryote, eukaryote, protist, fungus, invertebrate animal, vertebrate animal, microorganism, pathogen, agriculture pest, or any combination thereof. 111. The method of claim 97, wherein the cell is from a virus, prokaryote, eukaryote, protist, fungus, invertebrate animal, vertebrate animal, microorganism, pathogen, agriculture pest, or any combination thereof. 112. The method of claim 97, wherein the autocatalytic genome editing is used to target a pathogen. 113. The method of claim 112, wherein the pathogen is Plasmodium falciparum. 114. The method of claim 97, wherein the autocatalytic genome editing is used to target suppression of crop pests to those actively attacking a crop of interest. 115. The method of claim 97, wherein the autocatalytic genome editing targets a virus, retrovirus, or another disease independent of the type and stage of disease progression. 116. The method of claim 116, wherein the virus is HIV. 117. The method of claim 116, wherein the disease is cancer. 118. The method of claim 97, wherein the autocatalytic genome editing generates scoreable recessive mutant phenotypes in a single generation. 119. A construct for autocatalytic genome editing, the construct comprising: (a) a gene encoding an endonuclease, (b) at least one sequence encoding at least one guide polynucleotide, (c) an effector cassette, and (d) homology arms flanking the gene, the at least one sequence, and the cassette, wherein the homology arms directly abut the endonuclease cut site(s) determined by the at least one guide polynucleotide. 120. The construct of claim 119, wherein the endonuclease is a Cas protein. 121. The construct of claim 120, wherein the Cas protein is Cas9. 122. The construct of claim 119, wherein the guide polynucleotides are guide RNAs. 123. The construct of claim 119, wherein the at least one guide polynucleotide once expressed bind to the Cas9 protein and direct site directed cleavage of the genome at a specific site. 124. The construct of claim 119, wherein the sequence encoding the at least one guide polynucleotide is under a control of a separate promoter. 125. The construct of claim 124, wherein the separate promoter is an RNA-polymerase- I or III promoter. 126. The construct of claim 119, wherein the construct is injected as a DNA plasmid into a germline or introduced via DNA plasmid or viral expression vector into somatic cells of the organism to obtain a transgenic organism resulting in homozygous mutations or mutations passed on to progeny.
